summary: Discussion: 20/20 Program for Conserving 10 Acres along the Estero River, Village of Estero Conservation 20/20 Nomination 571. Action: No formal action recorded. Discussion: 20/20 Program for Conserving 10 Acres along the Estero River, Village of Estero Conservation 20/20 Nomination 571 Village Manager Sarkozy stated this property has been before Council previously and there's been an effort to secure 20/20 funding for this parcel from Lee County; led by the Conservation Foundation of the Gulf Coast. Debi Osborne, Director of Land Protection, is here to support the effort. : Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 7 of 8 Conservation Foundation of the Gulf Coast, Debi Osborne, pledged $15,000 and would be willing to continue fund raising. Village Manager Sarkozy spoke regarding maintenance and the cost of invasive exotics removal, which could be $30,000. Questions or Comments: The Council discussed the cost of removing exotics. After discussion, they suggested that Ms. Osborne go forward before the commissioners

summary: Public Works Projects Update. Action: No formal action recorded. Public Works Projects Update Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Public Works Director Willems presented a PowerPoint presentation to update current/future projects in the Village including Estero Parkway, Coconut Road Traffic Study, the bike/pedestrian master plan, US-41 median landscaping, Corkscrew Road/Lowes intersection, Parks and Recreation Master Plan, Estero on the River, water monitoring, and Stormwater Master Plan update at next Council Meeting, and major roadway repairs. Councilmember Levitan asked if the Council can create an ordinance to have testing to see if the septic system is failing. Village Council Meeting Agenda - February 20, 2019 Page 4 of 7 10. 11

project_title: Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board Items Recommended
summary: Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board Items Recommended. Action: e Reappoint Planning and Zoning Board members Marlene Naratil and James Tatooles for a one-year term expiring March 31, 2020. e Reappoint Design Review Board member Barry Jones for a one-year term expiring on March 31, 2020. e Appoint a Chairman for each Board. e Authorize staff to advertise for the three (3) vacant positions for Design Review Board, including a landscape architect per the Ordinance requirement. e Designate which Design Review Board member is considered the State-registered architect for purposes of determining a quorum. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs explained why this was being brought up to Council at this time. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan suggested the Council take each recommendation separately. Vice Mayor Errington expressed concern about not having a landscape architect. Community Development Director Gibbs explained that the Village will reach out to firms for assistance if needed

summary: Approval of Consultants Ranking for Estero River Sedimentation Analysis. Action: No formal action recorded. Village Public Works Director Willems provided comments regarding the ranking process and the reasons for selecting the consultant ranked as No. 1. summary: Property Assessed Clean Energy ("PACE") Program Resolution No. 2019-23 A Resolution of the Village ofEstero, Florida, (The "Village") Permitting Property Assessed Clean Energy ("PACE") Programs Within the Corporate Limits of The Village; Approving Agreeme. Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2019-23 as provided by Section 163.08, F.S., to authorize the PACE program in the Village ofEstero for commercial properties and execute the following agreements: 1) Party Membership Agreement to the Florida Green Finance Authority; 2) Limited Purpose Party Membership Agreement with the Florida Resiliency and Energy District; 3) Non-Exclusive Interlocal Subscription Agreement Relating to the Funding and Financing of Qualifying Improvements by the Florida PACE Funding Agency; and 4) Membership Agreement with the Green Corridor Property Assessment Clean Energy (PACE) District. In addition, authorized the Mayor or the Village Manager to sign the agreements

project_title: Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero
summary: Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero. Action: No formal action recorded. Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Options considered were: (1) locating adjacent to the Estero High School site on Williams Road; and, (2) building on a Three Oaks Parkway 70-acre site. Council Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Batos, Levitan, Wilson, McLain, Vice Mayor Errington and Mayor Ribble. Consensus ofthe Council indicated four in favor ofthe Three Oaks Parkway site and three in favor ofthe Williams Road location. Public Comment: Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe John Russell, Estero Palms The workshop session went into recess
